zh.lifeSummary: 當Deacon Edward Collins於1603年3月25日出生在英國素福爾可布拉福德時，他的母親Magdalene Treathone25歲，父親John Collins39歲。他於1628年11月20日與Martha Baylie在英國英格蘭素福爾可福拉苗林加姆結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他於1639年住在美國麻薩諸塞州。他去世於1689年4月9日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可哈爾斯托享年86歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可坎布利其。

nameMeanings.name: Collins
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Colin , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

Irish and Manx: shortened Anglicized from Ó Coileáin, compare Cullen , or in Man or west Ulster shortened from Mac Coileáin, compare McQuillan and McCallion . The genitival -s is a local addition to Collin and variants after the surname was Anglicized.
Americanized form of French   Colin   or   Collin   and also   Collette  .

variants.name: Colin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Alfredo, Francisco, Jesus, Ricardo, Alberto, Angel, Arturo, Eduardo, Esteban, Faustino.  French Alexandre, Andre, Armand, Georges, Henri, Jean Claude, Jean-Michel, Laurette, Lucien, Marcel, Patrice, Pierre.
French, Walloon, and Breton: from a French shortened pet form of the personal name   Nicolas   (see   Nicholas  ). This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
English: from the personal name Colin, a diminutive of Col, itself a pet form of Nicholas .

variants.name: Kolenc
meanings: Slovenian:
topographic name derived from koleno ‘knee’.
nickname from the dialect plant name kolenc ‘yellow bedstraw (Galium verum)’ or ‘common nipplewort (Lapsana communis)’. Compare Collins 3.

variants.name: Callins
meanings: Irish: variant of   Collins  .

variants.name: Collings
meanings: English: variant of Collins or Colling , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Deacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English deken, dicoun ‘deacon, a priest's assistant at church services’ (Old French diacne, Old English diacon, dēacon, both ultimately from Late Latin diaconus, from Greek diakonos ‘servant’). Since deacons were in holy orders they would not be permitted to marry (with allowance for those few already married when ordained), so the surname is probably in most cases a secular nickname rather than the name of a church official, perhaps denoting a deacon's servant.
English: variant of   Dicken  .

Irish (Wexford): of English origin (see 1 and 2 above), taken to Ireland in the 17th century. When the Irish surname is not of English origin, it may be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Deocáin ‘descendant of Deocán’, a personal name of uncertain etymology.

variants.name: Dakan
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Deacon  .
Possibly of Dutch origin, see   Dakin  .

variants.name: Iacono
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Salvatore, Giovanni, Sal, Vito, Angelo, Carmine, Gaetano, Gennaro, Giuseppe, Silverio, Aniello, Antonio, Leonardo, Pacifico, Rodolfo, Rosario, Salvadore, Ubaldo.
Italian (southern):
from iacono, a southern dialect form of diacono ‘deacon’; see Deacon .

variants.name: Deakin
meanings: variant of   Deacon  .
variant of   Dicken  .
English:

variants.name: Degon
meanings: Mainly North American: of uncertain origin. It is found among Jews as well as gentiles. Among the latter it is generally from the name of the English county, either directly or as a transferred use of the surname, which derives from a British tribal name said to mean ‘worshippers of the god Dumnōnos’.

variants.name: Dicken
meanings: Medieval pet form of Dick (with the Old French suffix -on), still in occasional use.

variants.name: Deakins
meanings: English: variant of Deacon , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
English: variant of   Dickens  .
In some cases possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Dekens: genitivized patronymic from a pet form of Diederik (see Dederick ).

variants.name: Deane
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which has a double origin. In part it is a local name for someone who lived in a valley (Middle English dene, Old English denu) and in part an occupational name for someone who served as a dean, i.e. ecclesiastical supervisor (Latin decanus). The given name also sometimes represents Italian Dino (a short form of names such as Bernardino), as in the case of the American actor and singer Dean Martin ( 1917–95 ).

variants.name: Deakyne
meanings: Probably English and Irish: variant of   Deacon  .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Deacon Edward Collins was born on 25 March 1603, in Bramford, Suffolk, England, his mother, Magdalene Treathone, was 25 and his father, John Collins, was 39. He married Martha Baylie on 20 November 1628, in Framlingham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 7 daughters. He lived in Massachusetts, United States in 1639. He died on 9 April 1689, in Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 86, and was buried in Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Deacon Edward Collins è nato il il 25 marzo 1603 nel Bramford, Suffolk, England, il suo madre, Magdalene Treathone, aveva 25 anni e il suo padre, John Collins, aveva 39 anni. Lui sposato Martha Baylie il 20 novembre 1628, nel Framlingham, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 7 figlie. Vissuto a Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1639. Morì il 9 aprile 1689, nel Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 86 anni, e fu sepolto a Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Deacon Edward Collinsが1603年3月25日にイギリススフォルクブラムフォードで生まれた時，母Magdalene Treathoneは25歳，父John Collinsは39歳であった。1628年11月20日にイギリスイギリススフォルクフラムリンガムでMartha Baylieと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1639年にはアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州に住んでいた。彼は1689年4月9日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスチャーレストウンで86歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスカンブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Deacon Edward Collins(이)가 1603년 3월 25일 영국 수폴크 브램퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 어머니 Magdalene Treathone(은)는 25세였고, 그의 아버지 John Collins(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1628년 11월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 프램링함에서 Martha Baylie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 1639년도에 미국 매사추세츠 주에서 살았다. 그는 1689년 4월 9일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 찰레스타운에서 86세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 캄브리지에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Martha Baylie em 20 de novembro de 1628, em Framlingham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele viveu em Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América em 1639. Ele faleceu em 9 de abril de 1689, em Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 86 anos, e foi sepultado em Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Deacon Edward Collins родился 25 марта 1603 г. в местности брамфорд, суффолк, Англия, его матери по имени Magdalene Treathone было 25 лет, а отцу, которого звали John Collins, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Martha Baylie 20 ноября 1628 г. в местности фрамлингхам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он жил в местности Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты в 1639 году. Он умер 9 апреля 1689 г. в местности чарльстоун, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 86 лет и был похоронен в местности камбридж, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Deacon Edward Collins nació el 25 de marzo de 1603, en Bramford, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su mamá, Magdalene Treathone, tenía 25 años y su papá, John Collins, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Martha Baylie el 20 de noviembre de 1628, en Framlingham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 7 hijas. Vivió en Masachusets, Estados Unidos en 1639. Murió el 9 de abril de 1689, en Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 86 años, y fue sepultado en Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
